user_query:
We’re adopting a lightweight learning loop for this workspace. There are prior one-line entries under input/.learnings and a new task brief in input/task-brief.md.

Please do the following strictly within output/:
1) Initialize output/.learnings/ with these files: errors.md, learnings.md, wishes.md, promotion-queue.md.
2) Merge the contents of input/.learnings/errors.md and input/.learnings/learnings.md into the corresponding files in output/.learnings/, deduplicating by stable id first (fallback: keyword) and preserving source labels. Specifically: bump the count for id:LRN-20260302-001 by +1; retain severity:critical for id:LRN-20260303-001; do not alter any entries’ source labels.
3) Do a pre-task review for the task described in input/task-brief.md using the keywords in input/keywords.txt. Create output/pre-task-review.md listing: (a) the relevant past entry IDs you found and (b) the concrete adjustments you’ll make to avoid repeat mistakes. Include a section header "Adjustments:".
4) Log at least one new error and one new learning related to this task into output/.learnings/errors.md and output/.learnings/learnings.md using the one-line format: [YYYY-MM-DD] id:ERR-YYYYMMDD-NNN | COMMAND | what failed | fix | … and [YYYY-MM-DD] id:LRN-YYYYMMDD-NNN | CATEGORY | what | action | … Include a source label and any applicable optional fields.
5) Queue candidates to output/.learnings/promotion-queue.md for any items that meet triggers (count:3+ or severity:critical) with status: pending. Do not queue any items with source:external.
6) Because you applied at least one prior learning during the pre-task review, increment its prevented:N counter in the corresponding entry.
7) Write a summary report to output/review-summary.json with this shape:
{
  "files": { "errors": <int>, "learnings": <int>, "wishes": <int>, "promotion_queue": <int> },
  "queued_pending": <int>,
  "total_prevented": <int>
}
Only write to output/ paths; do not modify anything outside output/.
